title: GE Aerospace Announces AerCap Selects GEnx Engines to Power 15 Additional Boeing 787 Dreamliners
author: Paul Bergman
description: AerCap Holdings, the world's largest owner of Boeing 787 aircraft, has selected GE Aerospace's GEnx-1B engine to power an additional 15 Boeing 787 Dreamliners. The GEnx engine has demonstrated a 99.98% dispatch reliability rate and stays on-wing at three times the rate of competing engines. GE Aerospace is investing over €110 million in European facilities and $1 billion across U.S. manufacturing sites to increase production capacity.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong order confirmation from major customer AerCap for 15 additional aircraft engines, demonstrating market confidence. Engine shows superior reliability metrics (99.98% dispatch rate) and significant operational achievements (50 million flight hours). Company is making substantial capital investments ($1+ billion) to expand production capacity, indicating strong demand and growth trajectory.

Continued investment in proven engine technology for fleet expansion demonstrates confidence in business model and customer demand. Selection of GEnx engines for additional 15 aircraft shows strategic commitment to reliable, economical aircraft platforms that support customer requirements.
Indirect positive sentiment as major aircraft lessor AerCap continues to order Boeing 787 Dreamliners equipped with GEnx engines, indicating sustained demand for Boeing's widebody aircraft platform.
Indirect positive sentiment as major aircraft lessor AerCap continues to order Boeing 787 Dreamliners equipped with GEnx engines, indicating sustained demand for Boeing's widebody aircraft platform.

title: This $4 Million Warrior Met Coal Sale May Be More About Profit Taking Than Coal Prices
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: High Ground Investment Management sold 41,297 shares of Warrior Met Coal (HCC) worth approximately $3.72 million in Q1 2026. Despite strong operational performance with Q1 revenue up 53% and EBITDA surging 263%, the sale appears to be profit-taking after the stock nearly doubled over the past year rather than a sign of fundamental deterioration. The company maintains strong production metrics and reaffirmed full-year guidance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q1 2026 operational results with 53% revenue growth, 263% EBITDA surge, record production volumes of 3.0 million short tons, and 14% reduction in cash costs. Stock up 83.9% over the past year. The fund sale is characterized as profit-taking into strength rather than a fundamental concern about the business.

Mentioned as a top holding of High Ground Investment Management (30.9% of AUM valued at $166.20 million) and as a stock recommended by The Motley Fool, but no specific news or analysis provided in the article.

title: AerCap Locks In Freighter Deal With Ethiopian Airlines
author: Lekha Gupta
description: AerCap Holdings shares rose in premarket trading following a significant freighter deal with Ethiopian Airlines for two Boeing 777-300ERSF converted freighters, with deliveries scheduled for Q2 2028. The company also recently ordered 100 Airbus A320neo Family aircraft with deliveries extending from 2028-2034. Technical analysis shows mixed momentum with neutral RSI but bearish MACD signals, while analysts maintain a Buy rating with an average price target of $128.77.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company secured a significant freighter deal with Ethiopian Airlines and recently ordered 100 Airbus aircraft, demonstrating strong business expansion and partnership growth. Stock up in premarket trading with analyst Buy rating and average price target of $128.77, though technical indicators show mixed momentum.
Boeing aircraft (777-300ERSF converted freighters) selected by AerCap for Ethiopian Airlines lease agreement, indicating continued demand for Boeing's cargo aircraft solutions.
Boeing aircraft (777-300ERSF converted freighters) selected by AerCap for Ethiopian Airlines lease agreement, indicating continued demand for Boeing's cargo aircraft solutions.
AerCap placed a substantial order for 100 Airbus A320neo Family aircraft with deliveries spanning 2028-2034, representing significant business for the aircraft manufacturer.

title: Globalstar Draws Investor Interest as Satellite Networks Extend Connectivity Beyond Cellular Coverage
author: Eric Trie
description: 140 Summer Partners LP acquired 917,500 shares of Globalstar (GSAT) for approximately $56 million, representing a new 4.14% position in the fund. The investment reflects growing investor interest in satellite communications as the company expands its low-Earth-orbit network to provide connectivity in remote areas where traditional cellular coverage is unavailable, serving maritime, energy, logistics, and emergency response sectors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong institutional investment from 140 Summer Partners LP, significant stock appreciation (171.8% over past year), expanding market opportunity in satellite communications for remote connectivity, and positioning as critical infrastructure for multiple industries including maritime, energy, and logistics.
Mentioned only as a top holding of 140 Summer Partners LP fund; no specific news or analysis provided about the company itself.

title: 140 Summer Partners Buys Acuity Stock
author: John Ballard
description: Investment fund 140 Summer Partners increased its position in Acuity Brands by 178,360 shares in Q4 2025, bringing its stake to 225,860 shares valued at $81.3 million (6% of AUM). Despite the stock underperforming the S&P 500 by 17.9 percentage points over the past year, the company showed strong growth with 13% revenue increase in fiscal 2025 and 20% quarterly growth, trading at a relatively low 14x expected earnings with projected 20% annualized earnings growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as the top holding of 140 Summer Partners LP ($148.1M, 10.9% of AUM) with no specific news or analysis provided in the article.
